channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Cuba still largely without power after nationwide grid collapse
item.description: Cuba remains largely without power a day after the country's grid collapsed, raising fresh questions about the island's generating system; officials gave no timeline for restoring service.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Over 100,000 people join protest rally in Belgrade against Serbia's president and government
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — At least 100,000 people descended on Belgrade on Saturday for a mass rally seen as a culmination of months-long protests against Serbia's populist President Aleksandar Vucic and his government.
item.dc:creator: JOVANA GEC and DUSAN STOJANOVIC, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: VOA journalists put on administrative leave after Trump axes parent agency
item.description: Journalists at Voice of America (VOA) were put on administrative leave on Saturday, a day after President Trump signed an executive order aimed at eliminating the U.S. Agency for Global Media (USAGM), VOA’s parent company.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Starmer challenges Putin to prove he is serious about peace by signing up to Ukraine ceasefire
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Saturday challenged Russian President Vladimir Putin to sign up to a ceasefire in its war against Ukraine if he is serious about peace, and said allies will keep increasing the pressure on the Kremlin, including by moving planning for a peacekeepi
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's pick for hostage negotiations envoy withdraws nomination but will continue on hostage issues
item.description: Adam Boehler, President Donald Trump’s nominee to become special envoy for hostage affairs, has withdrawn his nomination to avoid a requirement that he divest from his investment business.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Why Trump is singling out South Africa and accusing it of being anti-white and anti-American
item.description: The Trump administration’s decision to expel the South African ambassador is its latest move against a country it has singled out for sanctions and accused of being anti-white and anti-American.
item.dc:creator: Gerald Imray, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Cuba suffers another massive power outage leaving millions in the dark
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Cuba suffered a massive power outage Friday night affecting Havana and the country's provinces that left millions in the dark. Cuba’s Ministry of Energy and Mines said at around 8:15 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Thousands converging on Serbia's capital for a major rally that tests Vucic's populist government
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Tens of thousands of people swarmed the streets in Serbia's capital Belgrade on Friday, staging a joyful prelude to a major anti-government rally on the weekend and dealing a blow to populist President Aleksandar Vucic.
item.dc:creator: JOVANA GEC, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: State Department imposes sanctions on Thai officials after Uyghur men are deported to China
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The State Department on Friday announced sanctions on an unknown number of current and former Thai officials for their role in deporting at least 40 Uyghur men to China despite worries that they could be jailed or even face death there.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Killing site in Jalisco, Mexico just the latest in a long series of gruesome discoveries
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Prosecutors in western Mexico confirmed this week the discovery of hundreds of clothing items and bone fragments by a group of people searching for relatives at a previously known cartel training site, exposing major shortcomings in the original investigation.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA VERZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas says it will release a US-Israeli hostage and 4 bodies, but Israel expresses immediate doubt
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Palestinian militant group Hamas said Friday that it has accepted a proposal from mediators to release one living American-Israeli hostage and the bodies of four dual nationals who had died in captivity.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: House GOP promotes use of 'Taiwan' over 'Chinese Taipei' in slight to Beijing
item.description: A group of House Republicans is introducing legislation to assert Taiwan as the official name of the independently-governed island in U.S. communication, pushing back against claims to the territory by the Chinese government in Beijing. Rep. Byron Donalds (R-Fla.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: UN report warns Iran is stepping up electronic surveillance of women to enforce headscarf laws
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — Iran is increasingly relying on electronic surveillance and the public to inform on women refusing to wear the country's mandatory headscarf in public, as hard-liners push for harsher penalties for those protesting the law, a United Nations report released Friday found.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he's asked Putin to spare Ukrainian troops' lives
item.description: President Trump said he strongly requested that Russian President Vladimir Putin spare the lives of Ukrainian soldiers surrounded by Russian troops, in a statement posted to his social media site. Trump described “good and productive” discussions between U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: UN World Food Program to cut food aid for over 1 million people in Myanmar
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The United Nations food agency said on Friday that more than 1 million people in the war-torn nation of Myanmar will be cut off from food assistance due to critical funding shortfalls.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Europe's military personnel shortfalls exposed as Trump warns US security priorities lie elsewhere
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — In the year after Russia launched outright war on Ukraine, NATO leaders approved a set of military plans designed to repel an invasion of Europe. It was the biggest shake-up of the alliance’s defense readiness preparations since the Cold War.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Guatemala steps up patrols along border as US extends border security goals south
item.description: OCOS, Guatemala (AP) — As the United States government works to effectively extend its border security objectives south into Central America, countries like Guatemala have come under renewed scrutiny and pressure to step up their own border enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ontario Premier Ford hails 'positive' meeting with Lutnick
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford lauded his Thursday meeting with Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick as “positive” and “productive” after their public rift over tariffs on imported goods.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: UN official denies seeing 'a shred of evidence' showing staff in Gaza held hostages
item.description: Tom Fletcher, a top UN humanitarian aid official, says he has not seen "a shred of evidence" linking the international body's workers in Gaza to Hamas hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Poland calls on US to place nukes within its borders amid Russia threat
item.description: Polish President Duda has once again called on the US to place its nuclear arms within its borders to better deter Russia, mirroring talks with France amid security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel says Hamas sent a toddler to a military outpost
item.description: Israeli soldiers spotted a 4-year-old child approaching their military outpost, who was apparently sent by Hamas. Israel says the boy was returned to Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US lifts military freeze on Ukraine in push for 30-day ceasefire
item.description: The U.S. will immediately restore military aid and intelligence sharing to Ukraine following bilateral talks in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia, on Tuesday, with Ukraine expressing "readiness" to accept a 30-day ceasefire proposal. "Ukraine expressed readiness to accept the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: US-Canada products are 99% tariff-free: Canadian ambassador to US
item.description: Canada's ambassador to the United States said 99% of products traded between the two nations are tariff-free.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Canada's conservatives trying to associate Carney with Trudeau
item.description: Conservatives are on the defense after the Liberal Party picked Carney as Canada's next leader, a journalist says.
item.dc:creator: Connell McShane, NewsNation

item.title: Doctors declare Pope no longer in imminent danger, but remains hospitalized
item.description: Doctors said Monday that Pope Francis is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of pneumonia but have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Woman allegedly locked naked partner on balcony, leading to his death
item.description: Police say the woman locked her partner outside overnight after a fight amid temperatures of 38 degrees Fahrenheit.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Doctors declare Pope no longer in imminent danger, but remains hospitalized
item.description: Doctors said Monday that Pope Francis is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of pneumonia but have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope thanks volunteers for 'miracle of tenderness' as another Holy Year event passes without him
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis thanked volunteers Sunday for the “miracle of tenderness” they offer the sick, as he continued his treatment for double pneumonia and doctors reported some positive news.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and SILVIA STELLACCI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: See it: Activists vandalize Trump's Scotland golf resort
item.description: Pro-Palestinian activists said Saturday they vandalized one of Trump's golf resorts in Scotland in response to his proposal to empty the Gaza Strip of its Palestinian population.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 12 people injured by 3 men shooting randomly at Toronto pub customers
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — A dozen people were injured in a shooting at an eastern Toronto pub in what police called a reckless act of violence by three men who entered the bar and fired randomly without warning.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
